{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","@id":"http://localhost:3000/custom-kitchen-joinery/#faq","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"How much does custom kitchen joinery cost in Australia?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Custom kitchens range from $15,000 for a small budget kitchen with laminate doors and laminate tops, through to $120,000+ for a luxury kitchen with 2pac doors, stone benchtops, and butler's pantry. Most SteepWood kitchens fall between $35,000 and $75,000."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How long does it take to build a custom kitchen?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"From deposit to installation: typically 8 to 14 weeks. Design and approvals take 2–3 weeks; manufacturing 6–8 weeks; installation 3–7 days."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the difference between 2pac and polyurethane?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"2pac is a two-pack polyurethane — the same family of finish, sprayed in our spray booth for a hard, uniform finish. \"Polyurethane\" generally refers to the same material; the term \"2pac\" is the trade name. Both are durable, washable, and available in any colour from Dulux, Resene, or Polytec palettes."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can you match my kitchen joinery to my benchtop material?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes. We carry samples from Caesarstone, Smartstone, Essastone, Quantum Quartz, Polytec, and Laminex, and we coordinate door colour, benchtop, and splashback during the design stage."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Do you supply and install appliances?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"We design around your chosen appliances (or recommend Westinghouse, Bosch, Miele, Smeg, Fisher & Paykel depending on budget) and install joinery to suit. Appliance supply is optional — many clients prefer to source independently."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What warranties do you offer on kitchen joinery?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"10-year structural warranty on joinery, 25-year Blum hardware warranty (manufacturer-backed), benchtop warranties per supplier (Caesarstone offers a 25-year residential warranty)."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can you work with an existing floor plan, or does the whole kitchen need to change?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Both. We do straight cabinetry replacements within an existing footprint, and we also redesign full layouts in partnership with builders and architects."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the minimum space needed for a kitchen island?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"1,000 mm clear walkway on every side of the island is the practical minimum; 1,200 mm is ideal. Total island width including bench: from 900 mm for a small prep island, up to 1,500–1,800 mm for an island with seating."}}]}

{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","@id":"http://localhost:3000/built-in-wardrobes/#faq","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"How much does a built-in wardrobe cost in Australia?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Standard built-ins: $1,000–$2,500 per linear metre. Custom robes with internal drawers and LED: $3,000–$7,500. Walk-in robes: $10,000–$30,000+."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the minimum width for a walk-in robe?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"1,500 mm clear width for a single-sided WIR; 2,200 mm for a double-sided WIR with hanging on both walls and a 900 mm walkway."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Is it cheaper to build custom wardrobes or buy flatpack?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Flatpack (IKEA PAX) is cheaper upfront — $800–$1,500 for a comparable run. Custom is $3,000+. The trade-off is lifetime durability, ceiling-height usage, and a finish that holds up to daily wear. Custom typically pays back through resale value on a quality home."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What materials are used for built-in wardrobes?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Carcasses: Laminex/Polytec melamine. Doors: 2pac polyurethane, melamine, veneer, or solid timber. Hardware: Blum drawer runners and hinges."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How long does it take to install built-in wardrobes?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Design to install: 4–8 weeks. On-site installation typically 1–2 days."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Do built-in wardrobes add value to a home?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes — agents consistently cite quality joinery as a value-add. A $5,000 wardrobe upgrade typically returns $8,000–$12,000 at sale in mid-tier markets."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the difference between a built-in wardrobe and a walk-in robe?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"A built-in wardrobe is closed cabinetry with doors against a wall; a walk-in robe is a dedicated room with internal joinery. Walk-ins suit larger bedrooms; built-ins maximise smaller rooms."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can I get a wardrobe with integrated lighting and charging points?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes — Blum SERVO-DRIVE LED, motion-sensor strip lighting, and integrated USB-C/AC power points are standard options."}}]}

{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","@id":"http://localhost:3000/locations/newcastle/#faq","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"Where is SteepWood's workshop in Newcastle?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Our workshop is in Newcastle NSW 2300. We host design consultations by appointment."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Do you do joinery for heritage and Federation Newcastle homes?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes — we routinely match period mouldings, architraves, and door profiles for Hamilton, Cooks Hill, and The Hill heritage renovations."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the typical lead time for a Newcastle kitchen?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"8–12 weeks from deposit. Faster than Sydney/Melbourne projects because we don't need interstate logistics."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Do you work with Newcastle builders and architects?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes — we provide architect-grade shop drawings on request and work with local builders across the Hunter."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can you replace just the kitchen doors and benchtop without changing carcasses?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes — door + benchtop refresh is a common Newcastle project, ~$8,000–$18,000."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What suburbs of Newcastle do you cover for free in-home consultations?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"All Newcastle LGA, Lake Macquarie, Maitland, Cessnock LGAs at no charge. jsonLd:
{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","headline":"Kitchen Storage Planning for Australian Homes","description":"How to plan drawers, pantries, and internal layouts before your kitchen joinery quote — practical advice from a Newcastle workshop.","image":"http://localhost:3000/images/hero-workshop.svg","datePublished":"2026-06-06T04:55:01.678Z","dateModified":"2026-06-09T04:55:01.678Z","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"SteepWood Design Team"},"publisher":{"@type":"Organization","name":"SteepWood","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"http://localhost:3000/brand/steepwood-logo.png"}},"mainEntityOfPage":{"@type":"WebPage","@id":"http://localhost:3000/blog/kitchen-storage-planning-australia/"},"articleBody":"## Why storage planning comes first

Australian kitchens work hardest before breakfast. Between school lunches, bulk shopping, and bench appliances, storage decisions shape every other joinery choice.

<Callout variant="tip" title="Start with daily routines">
Map what you reach for every morning and every evening. That sequence should drive drawer heights, pantry widths, and corner solutions.
</Callout>

## Drawer banks vs cupboards

Deep drawers with Blum Legrabox or similar runners outperform standard cupboards for pots, lids, and prep bowls. Reserve overhead cupboards for lightweight items and display zones.

### Corner strategies

- Le Mans units for blind corners
- Magic corners where budget allows
- Open shelving only when access is unobstructed

## Pantry proportions

Walk-in pantries need 900–1200 mm clear depth. Butler''s pantries can be tighter if shelving is specified early.

<ServiceCTA service="custom-kitchen-joinery" />

## Measuring for a fixed-price quote

Bring appliance model numbers, plumbing positions, and power points to your design consult. Accurate dimensions reduce variations later.

<Callout variant="info">
SteepWood includes a free in-home measure before every fixed-price kitchen quote across our service areas.
</Callout>

## Next steps

Document your must-haves, nice-to-haves, and budget range before requesting a quote. That clarity shortens the design phase and improves first-pass accuracy.","wordCount":199,"timeRequired":"PT1M","url":"http://localhost:3000/blog/kitchen-storage-planning-australia/"}
